title: Agricultural Access to Substance Use Disorder Treatment and Mental Health Care Act of 2025
short_title: Agricultural Access to Substance Use Disorder Treatment and Mental Health Care Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill tells the government to study how hard it is for farmers and ranchers to get help with drug and alcohol problems and mental health care. The study will look at what makes it difficult for them to get treatment and find programs that work well. It will also give ideas on how to make it easier for farmers and ranchers to get the help they need. The government has two years to finish this study.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Agricultural Access to Substance Use Disorder Treatment and Mental Health Care Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Study on Accessibility of Substance Use Disorder Treatment and Mental Health Care Providers and Services for Farmers and Ranchers
sections.text: Within two years after this law is passed, the Comptroller General of the United States (the main government watchdog who reviews how government money is spent) must do a study about how easy it is for farmers and ranchers to get help for drug and alcohol problems and mental health care. The Comptroller General must then write a report about what they found and send it to the Secretary of Agriculture, the Secretary of Health and Human Services, and eight different committees in Congress that deal with farming, health care, and money issues. The study must look at five main topics. First, it must examine whether there are enough trained doctors, counselors, and other health care workers in rural areas who know how to help farmers, ranchers, farm workers, and their families with drug and alcohol problems and mental health issues. Second, it must identify the problems that make it hard for farmers and ranchers to get this kind of help, including not having enough money to pay for treatment, living too far from treatment centers, and cultural reasons that might prevent them from seeking help. Third, it must find examples of programs that work well in states and local communities that could be copied by the federal government to better serve farming communities. These successful programs could include hiring and training health care workers who understand farming life, teaching existing health care workers about farm culture, creating programs where farmers help other farmers, developing classes for young people in rural schools, expanding telehealth services that let people get care over the internet or phone, creating programs to reduce shame around mental health problems, helping mental health providers work better with farm organizations, and doing research to see which treatments work best for farmers and ranchers. Fourth, the study must look at how well the Farm and Ranch Stress Assistance Network program is being used and what the best practices are among the groups that receive money from this program. This network was created by a two thousand eight law to help farmers and ranchers deal with stress. Fifth, the study must make suggestions for how to make substance use disorder treatment and mental health care easier for farmers and ranchers to find and use.
